response.justification: Bogdan Szabo’s resume shows strong engineering leadership, distributed systems expertise, and hands-on experience with agentic workflows (MCP, AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq). However, he lacks direct production experience with multi-agent AI systems in regulated fintech environments, credit-risk modeling, or real-time financial decisioning. His fintech exposure is limited to pricing automation (Optilyz), not core infrastructure. While he excels in backend/distributed systems and team scaling, the role’s core demands—agentic AI for fintech trade decisioning, regulatory compliance, and hybrid engineering governance—are not demonstrably met by his experience.
strengths: Strong distributed-systems and backend expertise (event-driven platforms, microservices, real-time systems)
Proven track record scaling engineering teams and implementing CI/CD, TDD, and quality practices
Hands-on experience with AI-agent infrastructure (MCP, developer tooling for agentic workflows)
concerns: No demonstrated experience with multi-agent AI systems in high-stakes, regulated fintech environments
Limited exposure to financial infrastructure (credit risk, trade decisioning, liquidity optimization)
Lacks direct experience with hybrid engineering governance (outsourced teams, security baselines, compliance)

key_factors.factor: Multi-agent AI production experience in fintech
